problem_statement: In regulated workflows, Human-in-the-Loop is treated as the primary safety control but in production it becomes liability transfer: reviewers must approve high-volume probabilistic AI-driven actions they cannot independently verify at operational speed, decoupling accountability from control.
magnitude_evidence: Dave opens a queue of 200 items each morning with approximately 90 seconds per item; cryptographic retention windows of 5-30+ years
insufficiency_evidence: HITL frequently collapses under operational pressure: humans are asked to review probabilistic outputs that lack verifiable provenance at volumes incompatible with careful scrutiny; without meaningful human agency, oversight becomes rubber-stamping; humans under time pressure over-rely on automated suggestions, effectively negating the safety value of the loop (Parasuraman & Manzey, 2010)
solution_mechanisms: Minimum Viable HIC evidence record (Intent/Inputs/Constraints/Action Preview)
externalized deterministic policy engine (e.g. Open Policy Agent)
